abstract.p: This document defines the NWSViz EDR Service Profile, an OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR) Part 3 Service Profile. It specifies normative requirements and conformance tests for server implementations conforming to this profile.

localized-strings.localized-string: Terms, definitions, symbols and abbreviated terms
No terms and definitions are listed in this document.

This document uses the terms defined in https://portal.ogc.org/public_ogc/directives/directives.php[OGC Policy Directive 49], which is based on the ISO/IEC Directives, Part 2, Rules for the structure and drafting of International Standards. In particular, the word “shall” (not “must”) is the verb form used to indicate a requirement to be strictly followed to conform to this document and OGC documents do not use the equivalent phrases in the ISO/IEC Directives, Part 2.
This document also uses terms defined in the OGC Standard for Modular specifications (https://portal.opengeospatial.org/files/?artifact_id=34762[OGC 08-131r3]), also known as the 'ModSpec'. The definitions of terms such as standard, specification, requirement, and conformance test are provided in the ModSpec.
For the purposes of this document, the following additional terms and definitions apply.

The following documents are referred to in the text in such a way that some or all of their content constitutes requirements of this document. For dated references, only the edition cited applies. For undated references, the latest edition of the referenced document (including any amendments) applies.
There are no normative references in this document.

This document uses the terms defined in https://portal.ogc.org/public_ogc/directives/directives.php[OGC Policy Directive 49], which is based on the ISO/IEC Directives, Part 2, Rules for the structure and drafting of International Standards. In particular, the word “shall” (not “must”) is the verb form used to indicate a requirement to be strictly followed to conform to this document and OGC documents do not use the equivalent phrases in the ISO/IEC Directives, Part 2.
This document also uses terms defined in the OGC Standard for Modular specifications (https://portal.opengeospatial.org/files/?artifact_id=34762[OGC 08-131r3]), also known as the 'ModSpec'. The definitions of terms such as standard, specification, requirement, and conformance test are provided in the ModSpec.
For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in % additionally apply.

This document uses the terms defined in https://portal.ogc.org/public_ogc/directives/directives.php[OGC Policy Directive 49], which is based on the ISO/IEC Directives, Part 2, Rules for the structure and drafting of International Standards. In particular, the word “shall” (not “must”) is the verb form used to indicate a requirement to be strictly followed to conform to this document and OGC documents do not use the equivalent phrases in the ISO/IEC Directives, Part 2.
This document also uses terms defined in the OGC Standard for Modular specifications (https://portal.opengeospatial.org/files/?artifact_id=34762[OGC 08-131r3]), also known as the 'ModSpec'. The definitions of terms such as standard, specification, requirement, and conformance test are provided in the ModSpec.
For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in % and the following additionally apply.
No security considerations have been made for this document.

clause.title: Copyright notice
clause.p:
Copyright © 2026 Open Geospatial Consortium
To obtain additional rights of use, visit

clause.title: Note
clause.p: Attention is drawn to the possibility that some of the elements of this document may be the subject of patent rights. The Open Geospatial Consortium shall not be held responsible for identifying any or all such patent rights.

Recipients of this document are requested to submit, with their comments, notification of any relevant patent claims or other intellectual property rights of which they may be aware that might be infringed by any implementation of the standard set forth in this document, and to provide supporting documentation.

clause.title: License Agreement
clause.p: Use of this document is subject to the license agreement at

clause.title: Notice
clause.p: This document is not an OGC Standard. This document is an OGC Draft Standard and is therefore not an official position of the OGC membership. It is distributed for review and comment. It is subject to change without notice and may not be referred to as an OGC Standard.
Further, an OGC Draft Standard should not be referenced as required or mandatory technology in procurements.

abstract.title: Abstract
abstract.p: This document defines the NWSViz EDR Service Profile, an OGC API — Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR) Part 3 Service Profile. It specifies normative requirements and conformance tests for server implementations conforming to this profile.

clause.title: Keywords
clause.p: The following are keywords to be used by search engines and document catalogues.
ogcdoc, OGC API, EDR, Environmental Data Retrieval, NWSViz, service profile, weather

foreword.title: Preface
foreword.p: This document was prepared by NOAA/NWS/MDL.

clause.title: Security considerations
clause.p: No security considerations have been made for this document.

clause.title: Submitting Organizations
clause.p: The following organizations submitted this Document to the Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC):

clause.title: Submitters
clause.p: All questions regarding this document should be directed to the editor or the submitters:

clause.title: Scope
clause.p: This standard defines the NWSViz EDR Service Profile. It specifies requirements and conformance tests for implementations of this profile.

clause.title: Conformance
clause.p: Conformance with this standard shall be checked using the Abstract Test Suite in Annex A.
The following conformance classes are defined:

terms.title: Terms and definitions
terms.p: No terms and definitions are listed in this document.

This document uses the terms defined in OGC Policy Directive 49OGC Policy Directive 49, which is based on the ISO/IEC Directives, Part 2, Rules for the structure and drafting of International Standards. In particular, the word “shall” (not “must”) is the verb form used to indicate a requirement to be strictly followed to conform to this standard.
This document also uses terms defined in OGC API — EDR Part 1: Core.

classification.value: NWSViz EDR Service Profile Profile Standard

td.semx: NWSViz EDR Service Profile Profile Standard

description.p: The service SHALL declare conformance to OGC API Common, EDR, EDR Part 3, and OGC API Processes.

component.p: The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .

semx.p: The service SHALL declare conformance to OGC API Common, EDR, EDR Part 3, and OGC API Processes.
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .
The /conformance endpoint SHALL include .

description.p: The service SHALL provide a /collections endpoint listing all NWSViz collections.

component.p: The /collections endpoint SHALL return metadata for all 13 NWSViz collections.
Each collection SHALL include extent, data_queries, and output_formats.

semx.p: The service SHALL provide a /collections endpoint listing all NWSViz collections.
The /collections endpoint SHALL return metadata for all 13 NWSViz collections.
Each collection SHALL include extent, data_queries, and output_formats.

description.p: The position query SHALL return CoverageJSON.

component.p: Each collection SHALL provide a /collections/{id}/position endpoint.
The position endpoint SHALL accept a coords parameter containing a WKT POINT geometry.
The position endpoint SHALL return a valid CoverageJSON Coverage object as its default response.
The response Content-Type SHALL be application/prs.coverage+json.

semx.p: The position query SHALL return CoverageJSON.
Each collection SHALL provide a /collections/{id}/position endpoint.
The position endpoint SHALL accept a coords parameter containing a WKT POINT geometry.
The position endpoint SHALL return a valid CoverageJSON Coverage object as its default response.
The response Content-Type SHALL be application/prs.coverage+json.

description.p: The items query SHALL expose a virtual Zarr endpoint for each collection.

component.p: Each collection SHALL provide a /collections/{id}/items endpoint.
The items endpoint SHALL include an item with id zarr that acts as a virtual Zarr store.
The zarr item SHALL expose the underlying dataset as a Zarr store accessible via the .zarr/ path convention.
The zarr item SHALL be navigable using standard Zarr clients (e.g. xarray.open_zarr, zarr.open).
The zarr item response SHALL include a link with rel=zarr pointing to the root of the virtual Zarr store.
The virtual Zarr store SHALL reflect the full spatio-temporal extent of the collection.

semx.p: The items query SHALL expose a virtual Zarr endpoint for each collection.
Each collection SHALL provide a /collections/{id}/items endpoint.
The items endpoint SHALL include an item with id zarr that acts as a virtual Zarr store.
The zarr item SHALL expose the underlying dataset as a Zarr store accessible via the .zarr/ path convention.
The zarr item SHALL be navigable using standard Zarr clients (e.g. xarray.open_zarr, zarr.open).
The zarr item response SHALL include a link with rel=zarr pointing to the root of the virtual Zarr store.
The virtual Zarr store SHALL reflect the full spatio-temporal extent of the collection.

description.p: The service SHALL provide the edr-zarr-difference process for computing the difference between two EDR Zarr datasets.

component.p: The service SHALL expose a /processes/edr-zarr-difference endpoint.
The process SHALL accept collection_a, instance_a, collection_b, instance_b, parameter_name, zoom_level, crs, and unit as required inputs.
The process SHALL accept datetime as an optional input for valid time selection.
The process SHALL support sync-execute job control.
The process SHALL return a difference_result output containing the result of (A — B) as a virtual Zarr dataset.

semx.p: The service SHALL provide the edr-zarr-difference process for computing the difference between two EDR Zarr datasets.
The service SHALL expose a /processes/edr-zarr-difference endpoint.
The process SHALL accept collection_a, instance_a, collection_b, instance_b, parameter_name, zoom_level, crs, and unit as required inputs.
The process SHALL accept datetime as an optional input for valid time selection.
The process SHALL support sync-execute job control.
The process SHALL return a difference_result output containing the result of (A — B) as a virtual Zarr dataset.

description.p: The service SHALL provide the edr-zarr-difference-streaming process for streaming difference computation via a virtual Zarr endpoint.

component.p: The service SHALL expose a /processes/edr-zarr-difference-streaming endpoint.
The process SHALL accept collection_a, instance_a, collection_b, instance_b, parameter_name, and zoom_level as required inputs.
The process SHALL accept datetime, epsg, and units as optional inputs.
The process SHALL support sync-execute job control.
The process SHALL return a zarr_endpoint output containing a URL to stream difference chunks on-demand.

semx.p: The service SHALL provide the edr-zarr-difference-streaming process for streaming difference computation via a virtual Zarr endpoint.
The service SHALL expose a /processes/edr-zarr-difference-streaming endpoint.
The process SHALL accept collection_a, instance_a, collection_b, instance_b, parameter_name, and zoom_level as required inputs.
The process SHALL accept datetime, epsg, and units as optional inputs.
The process SHALL support sync-execute job control.
The process SHALL return a zarr_endpoint output containing a URL to stream difference chunks on-demand.

description.p: The service SHALL provide the edr-zarr-wind-streaming process for deriving wind speed and direction from u/v components via a virtual Zarr endpoint.

component.p: The service SHALL expose a /processes/edr-zarr-wind-streaming endpoint.
The process SHALL accept collection, instance, output_parameters, and zoom_level as required inputs.
The output_parameters input SHALL accept wind_speed, wind_direction, or both.
The process SHALL accept datetime, epsg, and units as optional inputs.
The process SHALL support sync-execute job control.
The process SHALL return a zarr_endpoint output containing a URL to stream wind data chunks on-demand.

semx.p: The service SHALL provide the edr-zarr-wind-streaming process for deriving wind speed and direction from u/v components via a virtual Zarr endpoint.
The service SHALL expose a /processes/edr-zarr-wind-streaming endpoint.
The process SHALL accept collection, instance, output_parameters, and zoom_level as required inputs.
The output_parameters input SHALL accept wind_speed, wind_direction, or both.
The process SHALL accept datetime, epsg, and units as optional inputs.
The process SHALL support sync-execute job control.
The process SHALL return a zarr_endpoint output containing a URL to stream wind data chunks on-demand.

references.title: Normative references
references.p: The following documents are referred to in the text in such a way that some or all of their content constitutes requirements of this document. For dated references, only the edition cited applies. For undated references, the latest edition of the referenced document (including any amendments) applies.

bibitem.formattedref: Mark Burgoyne, David Blodgett, Charles Heazel, Chris Little: OGC 19-086r6, OGC API — Environmental Data Retrieval Standard. Open Geospatial Consortium (2023). http://www.opengis.net/doc/IS/ogcapi-edr-1/1.1.0.
bibitem.title: OGC API
Environmental Data Retrieval Standard
OGC API — Environmental Data Retrieval Standard
bibitem.abstract:
The OGC API — Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR) standard provides a family of lightweight query interfaces to access spatiotemporal data resources by requesting data at a Position, within an Area, along a Trajectory or through a Corridor. A spatio-temporal data resource is a collection of spatio-temporal data that can be sampled using the EDR query pattern geometries. These patterns are described in the section describing the Core Requirements Class.
The goals of the EDR Application Programming Interface (API) that is specified by this standard are to:
Make it easier to access a wide range of data through a uniform, well-defined simple Web interface;
To achieve data reduction to just the data needed by the user or client while hiding much of the data storage complexity.
A major use case for the EDR API is to retrieve small subsets from large collections of environmental data, such as weather forecasts, though many other types of data can be accessed. The important aspect is that the requested data can be unambiguously specified by spatio-temporal coordinates.
The EDR API query patterns — Position, Area, Cube, Trajectory or Corridor — can be thought of as discrete sampling geometries, conceptually consistent with the feature of interest in the Sensor Observation Service (SOS) standard. A typical data resource accessed by an EDR API instance is a multidimensional dataset that could be accessed via an implementation of the Web Coverage Service (WCS) standard. In contrast to SOS and WCS, the EDR API is fully consistent with the patterns of the OGC API family of standards and aims to provide a single set of simple-to-use query patterns. Use cases for EDR range from real or virtual time-series observation retrievals, to sub-setting 4-dimensional data cubes along user-supplied sampling geometries. These query patterns do not attempt to satisfy the full scope of either SOS or WCS, but instead provide useful building blocks to enable the composition of APIs that satisfy a wide range of geospatial data use cases. By defining a small set of query patterns (and no requirement to implement all of them), the EDR API should help to simplify the design of systems (as they can be performance tuned for the supported queries) making it easier to build robust and scalable infrastructures.
With the OGC API family of standards, the OGC community has extended its suite of standards to include Resource Oriented Architectures and Web Application Programming Interfaces (APIs). These standards are based on a shared foundation, specified in OGC API-Common, which defines the resources and access paths that are supported by all OGC APIs. The resources are listed in Table 1. This document extends that foundation to define the EDR API.

bibitem.formattedref: OGC API — EDR Part 3: Service Profiles (draft)

component.p: Validate that conformance is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /conformance.
Verify the conformsTo array includes .
Verify the conformsTo array includes .
Verify the conformsTo array includes .
Verify the conformsTo array includes .

semx.p: Validate that conformance is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /conformance.
Verify the conformsTo array includes .
Verify the conformsTo array includes .
Verify the conformsTo array includes .
Verify the conformsTo array includes .

component.p: Validate that collections endpoint is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /collections.
Verify all 13 NWSViz collection ids are present in the response.
Verify each collection includes extent, data_queries, and output_formats.

semx.p: Validate that collections endpoint is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /collections.
Verify all 13 NWSViz collection ids are present in the response.
Verify each collection includes extent, data_queries, and output_formats.

component.p: Validate that position coveragejson is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /collections/{id}/position?coords=POINT(lon lat) for a valid coordinate within the collection extent.
Verify the response status is 200.
Verify the response Content-Type is application/prs.coverage+json.
Verify the response body is a valid CoverageJSON Coverage object with a type field equal to Coverage.
Verify the response domain contains the requested position.

semx.p: Validate that position coveragejson is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /collections/{id}/position?coords=POINT(lon lat) for a valid coordinate within the collection extent.
Verify the response status is 200.
Verify the response Content-Type is application/prs.coverage+json.
Verify the response body is a valid CoverageJSON Coverage object with a type field equal to Coverage.
Verify the response domain contains the requested position.

component.p: Validate that items zarr endpoint is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /collections/{id}/items.
Verify the response includes an item with id zarr.
Verify the zarr item includes a link with rel=zarr.
Send GET request to the zarr store root (.zarr/.zmetadata or .zarr/.zattrs).
Verify the response is valid Zarr metadata JSON.
Open the zarr store using xarray.open_zarr and verify the dataset dimensions and variables are accessible.
Verify the dataset spatio-temporal extent matches the collection extent.

semx.p: Validate that items zarr endpoint is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /collections/{id}/items.
Verify the response includes an item with id zarr.
Verify the zarr item includes a link with rel=zarr.
Send GET request to the zarr store root (.zarr/.zmetadata or .zarr/.zattrs).
Verify the response is valid Zarr metadata JSON.
Open the zarr store using xarray.open_zarr and verify the dataset dimensions and variables are accessible.
Verify the dataset spatio-temporal extent matches the collection extent.

component.p: Validate that process zarr difference is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /processes/edr-zarr-difference.
Verify the process description includes inputs collection_a, instance_a, collection_b, instance_b, parameter_name, zoom_level, crs, and unit.
POST a valid execution request to /processes/edr-zarr-difference/execution with two valid collection/instance pairs.
Verify the response status is 200.
Verify the response contains a difference_result output.

semx.p: Validate that process zarr difference is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /processes/edr-zarr-difference.
Verify the process description includes inputs collection_a, instance_a, collection_b, instance_b, parameter_name, zoom_level, crs, and unit.
POST a valid execution request to /processes/edr-zarr-difference/execution with two valid collection/instance pairs.
Verify the response status is 200.
Verify the response contains a difference_result output.

component.p: Validate that process zarr difference streaming is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /processes/edr-zarr-difference-streaming.
Verify the process description includes inputs collection_a, instance_a, collection_b, instance_b, parameter_name, and zoom_level.
POST a valid execution request to /processes/edr-zarr-difference-streaming/execution.
Verify the response contains a zarr_endpoint output with a valid URL.
Open the returned zarr_endpoint using xarray.open_zarr and verify the difference data is accessible.

semx.p: Validate that process zarr difference streaming is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /processes/edr-zarr-difference-streaming.
Verify the process description includes inputs collection_a, instance_a, collection_b, instance_b, parameter_name, and zoom_level.
POST a valid execution request to /processes/edr-zarr-difference-streaming/execution.
Verify the response contains a zarr_endpoint output with a valid URL.
Open the returned zarr_endpoint using xarray.open_zarr and verify the difference data is accessible.

component.p: Validate that process zarr wind streaming is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /processes/edr-zarr-wind-streaming.
Verify the process description includes inputs collection, instance, output_parameters, and zoom_level.
POST a valid execution request with output_parameters set to [wind_speed, wind_direction].
Verify the response contains a zarr_endpoint output with a valid URL.
Open the returned zarr_endpoint using xarray.open_zarr and verify wind_speed and wind_direction variables are present.

semx.p: Validate that process zarr wind streaming is correctly implemented.
Send GET request to /processes/edr-zarr-wind-streaming.
Verify the process description includes inputs collection, instance, output_parameters, and zoom_level.
POST a valid execution request with output_parameters set to [wind_speed, wind_direction].
Verify the response contains a zarr_endpoint output with a valid URL.
Open the returned zarr_endpoint using xarray.open_zarr and verify wind_speed and wind_direction variables are present.
